document.source_title: Madhav Tiwari: The ‘ziddi’ boy who only wanted to bat, but became a match-winner with the ball
document.summary: Madhav Tiwari, a 22-year-old cricketer for Delhi Capitals, has made a significant impact in IPL 2026, showcasing his skills as both a batsman and a bowler. Despite initially wanting to focus solely on batting, he has emerged as a match-winner, claiming key wickets and contributing crucial runs in tight situations. His journey from a reluctant bowler to a standout player highlights his determination and potential for future success.
key_takeaways: Madhav Tiwari made his IPL debut at 22, quickly becoming a key player for Delhi Capitals.
He scored crucial runs and took important wickets in his first two matches.
Madhav was initially focused on batting but was encouraged to bowl by his coach.
He has a reputation for thriving under pressure, earning the nickname 'kharaab situation' player.
Madhav's commitment to cricket is evident, having missed his sister's wedding for a tournament.
His performances suggest he could be a significant player for the future of Delhi Capitals.
document.why_it_matters: Madhav Tiwari's rise in IPL 2026 illustrates the potential of young cricketers in high-pressure environments, showcasing how talent can emerge from unexpected circumstances. His story emphasizes the importance of adaptability and resilience in sports, which can inspire other aspiring athletes.

claims.claim_text: Madhav Tiwari has become a match-winner for Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026.
claims.evidence_text: Madhav has already shown glimpses of why Delhi Capitals rate him so highly.

claims.claim_text: Madhav Tiwari was initially reluctant to bowl but has excelled in both batting and bowling.
claims.evidence_text: Bowling was never part of Madhav’s cricketing plan.

metrics.name: Strike Rate
metrics.evidence_text: He enjoyed a solid campaign in the 2024 edition of the Madhya Pradesh T20 League, batting at an impressive strike rate of 205.71.

metrics.name: Batting Average
metrics.evidence_text: He followed it up with another strong season in 2025, averaging 54 while maintaining a strike rate of 180.
